topic: Firmament Scaling Function from V12 geometry
change_summary: Verdict moved from 'Misleading' (c2, 2026-04-07) to 'Self-Contradicted' (current). detail_evidence expanded from 1208 → 2464 chars (+104%) adding check_eclipse_fsf.py formula, version-instability evidence (V51 ~3% vs V50.6 ~29% HAD error, BOU radius shift 5715→9983), and INTERMAGNET/eclipse framing. tldr fields added. However, finding, detail_verdict_text, tldr_verdict, and tldr_evidence all still read like Misleading-era text — they describe 'internal derivation ≠ confirmation' rather than the self-contradiction(s) that justify the new verdict.
our_argument_summary: The finding/verdict-text framing remains the Cycle-2 'Misleading' argument (internal derivation tests consistency, not truth; eclipse still pending). The new Self-Contradicted verdict is carried by two evidence threads: (a) version-instability inside detail_evidence — the same FSF values that reproduce to ~3% under V51 ellipse H(r) with V51 radii do NOT reproduce under V50.6 exponential H(r) with V50.6 radii (HAD 1.89 vs published 1.47), and the baseline station BOU shifts from r=5715 (V50.6) to r=9983 (V51), so 'derived from V12 geometry' is version-unstable; and (b) Section 1.8's coordinate collapse — FSF derives directly from H(r), which was itself calibrated from Polaris observations through the n(r) aetheric medium the refraction fix invokes, so FSF cannot be evaluated without resolving a ray-tracing geometry the dome's coordinate system no longer supports. Part 2b cross-references this in its derivation-question paragraph. The two self-contradiction arguments are coherent, but WIN-044's own verdict-summary text does not invoke either — it still delivers the old Misleading punchline.

relevant_files: raw-text/03-predictions.txt (lines 21–34: FSF definition and E001–E009 values)
raw-text/05-model.txt (H(r) = 8537 × exp(−r/8619) km; refraction fix line 38; n(r) definition)
referenced but not in raw-text/: check_eclipse_fsf.py, check_fsf.py, monitor.py Domain 10 line 515 (ΔB = baseline × coverage × FSF)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: FSF(r) = (H(r)/r) / (H(r_BOU)/r_BOU) per dome text. With H(r) = 8537 × exp(−r/8619), this simplifies to (r_BOU/r) × exp((r_BOU − r)/8619). Station ΔZ is then baseline × coverage × FSF with two baselines (BOU 2017: −10.9 nT; W004 empirical: −22.24 nT).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The E001–E009 predictions (FSF values, coverage, ±2σ) are listed as static text in 03-predictions.txt. No live monitoring. Verification against INTERMAGNET must wait for the August 12, 2026 eclipse, which has not occurred.
data_sources_used: INTERMAGNET (future — data does not yet exist for this event)
Station radii r_k (derived via V12 r·tan(θ_Polaris) = H(r) — same coordinate system destabilized in Section 1.8)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Dual baselines (BOU −10.9 vs W004 −22.24 nT) produce an effective ~2× prediction range per station. Combined with ±2σ, the prediction windows are wide (e.g., E001 Ebro spans roughly −8.6 to −23.8 nT), weakening discriminatory power even after the eclipse.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None in WIN-044's own detail_evidence. HOWEVER: Part 2b writes 'FSF(r) = exp(−r/λg)' in its derivation discussion, which is not the dome's formula. The dome's FSF = (H(r)/r) / (H(r_BOU)/r_BOU) — the correct ratio includes 1/r and BOU normalization. This is a Part-2b defect, not a WIN-044 defect, but WIN-044 cross-references that part and the inconsistency is visible to any reader who clicks through.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No domain fetches INTERMAGNET or any eclipse magnetometer data. The E001–E009 numbers are hardcoded text in 03-predictions.txt. monitor.py Domain 10 is referenced as the consumer of FSF but no live fetch exists against the August 2026 eclipse because that event has not occurred. 'none' is correct.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard ionospheric physics explains eclipse magnetic perturbations via Sq current modulation driven by latitude-dependent E-region conductivity changes. The dome's FSF is a geometric ratio with no standard counterpart. The relabel concern is softer and subtler: FSF(r) may incidentally correlate with geomagnetic latitude, so an apparent 'match' to eclipse data could reflect standard-physics latitude structure rather than dome geometry — but that is a causal-confound critique, not a relabel. 'false' is correct.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Predictions E001–E009 were registered in Git + OpenTimestamps on 2026-03-12, before the August 12, 2026 test. This is a pre-registered future-event prediction, not a retrofit to known data. CAVEAT: the registration timestamps the observation-and-prediction bundle in a prediction table; OTS anchoring alone does not fix the wrong-side problem (see Timestamp Error tab), but that is a methodological concern, not a post_hoc one. 'false' stays.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Structurally yes: FSF = (H(r)/r) / (H(r_BOU)/r_BOU), with H(r) = 8537 × exp(−r/8619) (V12 parameters). A derivation path from dome parameters to station amplitudes exists. HOWEVER, the r_k values for each station come from r·tan(θ_Polaris) = H(r), which Section 1.8 shows depends on Polaris elevation through the n(r) medium — the same medium used to fix the sun-altitude collision. And the post_hoc_evidence string on the live tag object still reads 'Genuine dome-derived prediction with future empirical test' — that is the Cycle-2 Misleading framing and directly contradicts the current Self-Contradicted verdict. The tag is still technically 'true' because the formula is structurally dome-geometric, but the supporting string needs a rewrite.

holes_found.description: Verdict–evidence narrative mismatch. The verdict is 'Self-Contradicted' but detail_verdict_text reads 'Internal model derivations are not independent predictions; they test consistency, not truth.' That sentence describes a Misleading-category problem (derivation ≠ confirmation), not a self-contradiction. A dome defender can and will say: 'Your label says Self-Contradicted, your explanation says it's not independent. Those are different objections. Which is it?' The detail_evidence does establish genuine self-contradiction (version-instability, coordinate dependence on a contaminated medium), but the summary readers see does not.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite detail_verdict_text to invoke one or both self-contradiction mechanisms explicitly. Draft: 'The FSF's predicted values are version-unstable — the same dome geometry gives different station-specific FSF depending on which H(r) and which BOU radius is current. A formula whose numerical output depends on which internal parameters are live is not a derivation from dome geometry; it is a fit. Compounded, Section 1.8 shows the coordinate system that assigns station radii is destroyed by the refraction fix the dome uses to rescue the sun.'

holes_found.description: finding field has the same problem. It reads 'Internal model derivation claimed as confirmed WIN before observational test (eclipse August 2026); tests self-consistency, not truth.' This is the Cycle-2 Misleading finding verbatim. Under the new Self-Contradicted verdict the finding should describe the self-contradiction, not the 'pending eclipse' framing.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace finding with something like: 'FSF is version-unstable: same dome geometry produces different station-specific values under V50.6 vs V51, and the coordinate system that assigns station radii is destroyed by the dome's own refraction fix (Section 1.8).'

holes_found.description: tldr_verdict and tldr_evidence both still frame the problem as 'derived from own assumptions → tests consistency, not truth.' Readers who never expand will see the old Misleading argument attached to a new Self-Contradicted verdict. Per curmudgeon.md §TLDR Review ('A TLDR that contradicts the evidence/verdict it summarizes is major') this is a TLDR mismatch.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite both TLDRs to match the verdict. tldr_verdict draft: 'The same dome parameters produce different FSF values in different dome versions, and the station radii the formula needs come from a coordinate system the dome's refraction fix just invalidated.' tldr_evidence draft: 'The formula looks geometric, but its numbers depend on which version of the dome you use — HAD computes to 1.47 in V51 and 1.89 in V50.6 from the same underlying dome. And the station radii it plugs in come from Polaris angles that travel through the refractive medium the dome added to save the sun.'

holes_found.description: code_analysis.post_hoc_evidence string is stale. It reads: 'Derived from dome V12 geometry (Firmament Scaling Function H(r)/r). Eclipse path predictions registered before August 2026 test window. Genuine dome-derived prediction with future empirical test.' The closing phrase 'Genuine dome-derived prediction with future empirical test' explicitly contradicts the current Self-Contradicted verdict. This field is supposed to explain why post_hoc is false, but it is being used to re-assert the old Misleading framing.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten to just the post_hoc justification: 'Predictions E001–E009 registered in Git + OpenTimestamps 2026-03-12, before the August 2026 test window. Not a retrofit to observed data.' Move any 'dome-derived' commentary into derives_from_dome_evidence, where it can also flag the coordinate-collapse caveat.

holes_found.description: Code analysis block is missing monitoring_evidence, relabels_standard_evidence, and derives_from_dome_evidence fields. Only post_hoc_evidence exists. The curmudgeon schema (curmudgeon.md §5, §7) requires per-tag evidence strings, and the reviewed flag is already true, so these are just unfilled slots.
holes_found.recommendation: Populate the three missing *_evidence fields on next patch. The strings I wrote in code_analysis_tags above are drop-in candidates.

holes_found.description: Cross-reference to the coordinate collapse argument (Section 1.8) is absent from WIN-044's own text. Section 1.8 explicitly lists WIN-044 among the contaminated WINs, and Part 2b's derivation-question paragraph asserts Section 1.8 self-contradicts the FSF — but the WIN detail_evidence does not link back. A reader who starts on the WIN row has no path to the structural argument that now carries the verdict.
holes_found.recommendation: Insert a single sentence at the end of detail_evidence (or in detail_extra) saying something like: 'See Section 1.8 — the r values this formula plugs in are derived from Polaris elevation through the same aetheric medium the dome invokes to fix the sun, so the geometric ratio is evaluated on contaminated inputs.'

holes_found.description: Dual-baseline tolerance not quantified anywhere in WIN-044. The two baselines (BOU −10.9 vs W004 −22.24 nT) produce ~2× prediction windows per station, so even a successful eclipse test will be weakly discriminating. This was also flagged in c2 and remains unaddressed.
holes_found.recommendation: Keep as a note for detail_extra if it is ever populated. Optional — the two major findings dominate.

holes_found.description: detail_extra is still null. c2 recommended a short mathematical exposition (formula, station values 0.642–2.075, baseline structure). Not implemented. Lower priority than the verdict-alignment issues above.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional — would strengthen perceived rigor, not load-bearing for verdict.

stronger_arguments.description: Lead with version-instability. The 1.47 (V51) vs 1.89 (V50.6) HAD swing is a textbook self-contradiction: the dome cannot claim a formula as 'derived from V12 geometry' when the same geometry produces two incompatible numbers across versions. This is cleaner and more publicly accessible than the Section 1.8 coordinate-collapse chain.

Then connect to Section 1.8 as the deeper mechanism: version-instability is a symptom; the underlying problem is that the dome's coordinate system is calibrated through a refractive medium whose existence is asserted post-hoc to fix the sun-altitude collision. This turns two separate critiques into a single reinforcing argument.

Preempt the 'derivation ≠ test' philosophical framing. We should say explicitly that we are NOT calling WIN-044 wrong because the eclipse hasn't happened — we are calling it self-contradicted because its derivation inputs are unstable and upstream-invalidated. The Misleading-era framing should be explicitly retired in WIN-044 text to prevent the dome defender from claiming our verdict is philosophical hairsplitting.

kernel_of_truth.description: WIN-044 is structurally the dome model's most intellectually honest attempt at a genuine prediction: the FSF is constructed from the published H(r) function, applied uniformly to nine observatories, pre-registered in August 2026, and linked to a concrete pending dataset (INTERMAGNET). Compared to the Schumann or toroidal relabelings, this is real mathematical work.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Self-contradiction bites at two levels: the numerical output depends on which dome version is live (so 'the' derivation from V12 geometry does not exist as a single object), and the coordinate inputs the formula consumes are contaminated by the same refractive medium the dome invokes to patch a different collision elsewhere in the model. The effort is real; the result is internally inconsistent.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Constructed as a dome defender who has accepted the review's own principles: 'Your Self-Contradicted verdict is label inflation. The c2 reviewer correctly identified WIN-044 as Misleading — derivation-before-test is an epistemic category, not a contradiction. Your new evidence is (a) version changes between V50.6 and V51 (that is how models evolve — we refined H(r) and station radii; asking us to call revision a contradiction is asking every scientific paper to stay at V1 forever) and (b) a cross-reference to Section 1.8, which relies on the premise that n(r) is strong enough to contaminate Polaris angles meaningfully — but Polaris is nearly overhead at high latitudes where n(r) is weakest (~1.08). The contamination integral is smallest exactly where the dome calibrates. Meanwhile your own summary text admits we are doing something novel: deriving FSF from published parameters, pre-registering, pointing to independent data. That is how theories work. Call us Pending, not Self-Contradicted.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Three specific text changes inside WIN-044 will neutralize this defense: (1) Explicitly state in detail_verdict_text that the self-contradiction is version-instability + upstream coordinate contamination, NOT 'derivation ≠ test'; retire the Misleading-era language. (2) Include the specific numbers that make version-instability concrete — HAD 1.47 (V51) vs 1.89 (V50.6), BOU r_BOU 5715 → 9983. A version change that swings a key station's FSF by 29% is not refinement, it is the formula claiming different values from the same dome. (3) Make the coordinate-collapse cross-reference explicit with a quantitative hook: at the latitude of BOU, H(r=9983) = 2,685 km and n(9983) ≈ 1.44 — the refractive contrast with atmosphere is ~1,500×. Polaris contamination is not a boundary-case worry; it is the bulk of the medium. Together these fix the rhetorical vulnerability without changing the verdict.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Three real issues worth tracking, one of which is inside WIN-044 itself. (1) INTERNAL: WIN-044's own code_analysis.post_hoc_evidence calls the prediction 'Genuine dome-derived prediction with future empirical test' while the verdict is Self-Contradicted — the tag string and the verdict tell different stories. (2) CROSS-SECTION: Part 2b writes the FSF as 'FSF(r) = exp(−r/λg)' in its derivation-question paragraph. That is not the dome's formula — the correct form is (H(r)/r)/(H(r_BOU)/r_BOU) ≈ (r_BOU/r) × exp((r_BOU − r)/8619). The 1/r factor and BOU normalization are missing. Any reader who jumps between WIN-044 and Part 2b sees two different FSFs. This is a Part-2b defect, not a WIN-044 defect, but it affects cross-WIN consistency. (3) CROSS-WIN (WIN-056): Section 1.8 cites WIN-056 (solar elevation from H(r)) as the anchor of the refraction problem. WIN-044 inherits H(r) from the same construction. The two WINs should use matching self-contradiction language; WIN-044's verdict-summary still frames its problem as a Misleading-category issue, which will look inconsistent beside WIN-056's framing.

claims_checked: Refractive contrast with Earth's atmosphere (natm = 1.000293): ~300× at r=3000, ~6000× at ice wall
H(6114 km) ≈ 4,200 km (firmament ceiling at sun's orbital radius)
FSF V51 reconstruction for HAD against published 1.471
BOU r_BOU shift V50.6 → V51: 5,715 → 9,983 km
quantitative_verification.computations: Computed in python3 (see curmudgeon run log). Results: n(3000)=1.083; n(10000)=1.438; n(20015)=2.840; n(Tropic r≈7,874)=1.299; refractive contrast 273× at r=3,000 and 6,280× at ice wall; r such that H(r)=4,200 is r=6,114 km; for V51 r_HAD ≈ 8,200 km the computed FSF is 1.497 (published 1.471, within ~2%). All Section 1.8 quantitative claims reproduce within rounding. The Tropic of Cancer value the review rounds to 1.27 computes to ~1.30 at a Polaris-derived r of ~7,874 km — close enough that the review's '1.27 at the Tropic of Cancer' reads as a minor rounding or different-r choice, not a real error.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: The Tropic of Cancer n(r) value of 1.27 in Section 1.8 computes to 1.30 on first-pass with the V12 radius derived from θ_Polaris = 23.5°. Rounding or a slightly different radius definition. Not a WIN-044 issue — it is a Section 1.8 number — but flagged here so the decider can confirm the choice when it touches 1.8. The HAD V50.6 vs V51 numbers in WIN-044 detail_evidence I could not recompute because V50.6 station radii are not in the current raw-text; the claim relies on check_fsf.py outputs that are asserted in text. Worth having the analyst confirm those values against the version-instability expansion package that fed this verdict change.

deeper_analysis: The verdict change from Misleading → Self-Contradicted is defensible, but the rewrite was incomplete: the evidence section was expanded with the self-contradiction material (version-instability, check_eclipse_fsf.py derivation, BOU radius shift) while the one-line claim, finding, detail_verdict_text, and both TLDRs were left in their c2 Misleading form. The result is a WIN whose label does not match its summary. The Cycle-2 review (2026-04-07) correctly pegged the older text as Misleading; the Cycle-3 decider re-verdicted without retiring that language, and the current file reads as a half-finished edit. Either the verdict should roll back to Misleading (weaker but internally consistent), or the summary text should be rewritten to carry the self-contradiction. Because (a) the detail_evidence now contains real self-contradiction material (version-instability is a textbook example), (b) Section 1.8's coordinate-collapse argument independently justifies the stronger verdict, and (c) Part 2b explicitly cross-references both, the right move is to keep Self-Contradicted and finish the rewrite rather than retreat. Opposing view I should note: a fair-minded dome defender can argue that version-instability is how science works (refinement is not contradiction) and that Section 1.8's coordinate-collapse claim relies on n(r) being large where Polaris is observed — which is the weakest part of the argument, because near the north pole n(r)≈1.08 and Polaris is nearly overhead, minimizing path length through the refractive medium. The review should explicitly acknowledge this and answer it (the contamination that matters is integrated along slant paths from low-latitude observatories, not near-pole ones — and the dome's station radii span r = 3,000 to 10,000 km where n ranges 1.08–1.44 along non-trivial slant paths). Without that preemption WIN-044 is rhetorically more brittle than the Self-Contradicted label implies.

citations_verified: raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines 21–34 — FSF definition 'field strength factor from V12 H(r)/r dome geometry' and E001–E009 station-specific FSF/coverage/predicted ΔZ values (verified against current dome source).
INTERMAGNET (https://www.intermagnet.org) — active observatory network, exists as described.
H(r) = 8537 × exp(−r/8619) — verified in raw-text/05-model.txt; all n(r) and H(r) numbers in Section 1.8 reproduce numerically within rounding.
citations_failed: check_eclipse_fsf.py and check_fsf.py are cited in detail_evidence as the source of the FSF = (H(r)/r)/(H(r_BOU)/r_BOU) definition and the V51 vs V50.6 reproduction claims (3% vs 29% HAD error). Those files are not present in raw-text/ — the claim is carried on the analyst's expansion package, not a primary dome source we can inspect. Decider should confirm before this becomes a publicly load-bearing citation.
citations_unchecked: monitor.py Domain 10 line 515 (ΔB = baseline × coverage × FSF) — not in raw-text/; cited in detail_evidence but not verifiable from current clone.
